feedback.text: It already happened a few times to me, that a game I payed for would not be playable anymore. In some cases publishers turned off the servers, in others the game had been removed from the digital store I bought it from. While most stores still give access to the files after the games had been delisted, in other cases (like "Gods Remastered" bought at the Microsoft store) the game had been removed for good and could not even be installed after that. Refund? No sir, not gonna happen. I could not even make a backup copy of the game since MS encrypts installation files. It's annoying that we paying customers get treated like that while others who get the games in a not so legal way just laugh at our problems. MMO servers being shut down are another problem. Some of the games I payed good money for (for a big collector's edition box), in the future will be lost. At least I will have the collector's edition box, but the game will be gone. Something needs to be done about this. We used to 'buy' stuff that after the purchase was ours. That changed drastically, currently we own nothing.
